downstream_consumers: RAG index (semantic header content queryable after re-index)

risks.title: Duplicate header blocks created by --source-headers
risks.mitigation: Run deduplicate-headers.mjs after every --source-headers batch. The deduplication script was written specifically for this scenario (post WO-SELF-SCAN-GAP-REMEDIATION-001).

risks.title: Invalid @layer value on annotated files
risks.mitigation: Check layer assignment before running populate: cli→src/cli/, tooling→scripts/, test→__tests__/. Validate with populate-coderef after each batch.

risks.title: Fixture test files accidentally annotated
risks.mitigation: Manually exclude __tests__/fixtures/ and any test file that embeds @coderef-semantic as string literals in test data. Review diff before committing.

risks.title: Coverage target not met after Phase 2 (>50 missing)
risks.mitigation: Phase 2 includes a full-suite populate run + manual annotation loop for long-tail files. If target not met, extend Phase 2 rather than accepting the gap.

dependencies: scripts/deduplicate-headers.mjs — safety check for duplicate blocks post-annotation
src/pipeline/semantic-header-parser.ts — parser that detects new headers

decision_records.decision: Use populate-coderef --source-headers as primary annotation mechanism
decision_records.rationale: populate-coderef already knows element boundaries and layer assignments. Manual annotation would be error-prone at 76 files. The CLI handles the header format correctly.

decision_records.decision: Phase 1 targets src/cli/ only (7 files, ~80 elements)
decision_records.rationale: CLI files are user-facing and directly RAG-queryable. They provide the highest intelligence value per annotation effort. Phase 2 handles scripts/ and __tests__/ which are less frequently queried.

decision_records.decision: Skip fixture test files that embed @coderef-semantic as string data
decision_records.rationale: These files contain the header string as test input, not as a real semantic header. Annotating them would create duplicate/conflicting blocks.
